name: suncalc

en.when_to_use: Use it to calculate and visualize sunrise/sunset, dawn/dusk, golden hour, solar azimuth/altitude, moonrise/moonset, moon phase, and illuminated fraction for a place and date (sunlight analysis, solar paths, photography golden hour, moon-phase dials): a date plus latitude/longitude returns the full set of times and angles. d3-celestial in the catalog renders star maps/celestial projections but does not calculate specific times or bearings, so the two complement each other; not for high-precision professional ephemerides, which need a specialist astronomy library.
en.why_valuable: Sunrise/sunset, golden hour, solar azimuth/altitude, and moon phase depend on astronomical algorithms (Julian dates, ecliptic->equatorial->horizontal coordinate transforms, and atmospheric-refraction correction). AI-reported values are often off by tens of minutes or even use the wrong date or polar-day/night state; suncalc maps date plus latitude/longitude directly to solar/lunar times and position angles with compact, widely validated formulas.

data_in.note: Input is always (date: Date, lat: number, lng: number): latitude is positive north/negative south and longitude positive east/negative west, in degrees. The date is treated as a UTC instant without local time-zone conversion; getTimes uses only its date part to determine the "solar day" (hours/minutes/seconds do not affect results). Important: v2.0.1 is the breaking v2 API--angles are in degrees, and azimuth is clockwise from north (0=N,90=E,180=S,270=W). This is the exact opposite of the v1 "radians + measured from south" behavior many people remember; do not interpret output using the old behavior (verified in actual source index.js lines 42/97: degrees, north-based). At polar latitudes some times are null with alwaysUp/alwaysDown flags. license: the npm license field is empty, but the repository LICENSE is BSD-2-Clause.

schema.date: Date -- UTC instant; getTimes uses only its date part to determine the solar day
schema.lat: number -- latitude in degrees, positive north and negative south
schema.lng: number -- longitude in degrees, positive east and negative west
schema.height?: number -- observer elevation in meters, used only by getTimes, default 0

when_to_use: 要算"某地某天"的日出/日落/黎明黄昏/黄金时刻、太阳方位角高度角、月出月落/月相月照率并可视化(日照分析、太阳轨迹、拍摄黄金时刻、月相盘)时用它:输入日期+经纬度直接吐全套时刻与角度。目录里的 d3-celestial 是渲染星空/天球投影图,不算具体时刻/方位,二者互补;非需要高精度专业星历(那需专业天文库)。
why_valuable: 日出日落/黄金时刻/太阳方位高度/月相靠天文算法(儒略日、黄道→赤道→地平坐标变换、大气折射修正),AI 徒手报这些时刻常差几十分钟甚至弄错日期或极昼极夜;suncalc 用一套紧凑、被广泛验证的公式把日期+经纬度直接映射成太阳月亮的时刻与位置角。

data_in.note: 输入永远是 (date: Date, lat: number, lng: number):纬度北正南负、经度东正西负,单位度。日期按 UTC 瞬时处理、不做本地时区换算,getTimes 只看日期部分定"太阳日"(时分秒不影响结果)。关键:v2.0.1 是 v2 破坏性 API——角度单位为『度』、azimuth 为『北基顺时针』(0=N,90=E,180=S,270=W);这跟很多人记忆里 v1 的『弧度 + 从南起算』完全相反,别按旧记忆解读输出(已核对真实源码 index.js line 42/97,确为度、北基)。极纬度下部分时刻为 null 并置 alwaysUp/alwaysDown 标志。license:npm license 字段为空,但仓库 LICENSE 实为 BSD-2-Clause。

schema.date: Date —— UTC 瞬时;getTimes 只取其日期部分定太阳日
schema.lat: number —— 纬度,北正南负,单位度
schema.lng: number —— 经度,东正西负,单位度
schema.height?: number —— 观测者海拔(米),仅 getTimes 用,默认 0

provides._note: headless-primitive:纯天文计算,不渲染、无 L 级、无 carriers 矩阵;消费 AI 拿到时刻/角度后自行画轨迹/时刻轴/月相盘。
provides.outputs: getTimes → {sunrise,sunset,dawn,dusk,goldenHour,goldenHourEnd,solarNoon,nadir,…}(值为 Date|null;solarNoon/nadir 恒为 Date);getPosition → {azimuth,altitude}(度、北基);getMoonIllumination → {fraction(0..1),phase(0..1),angle,waxing};getMoonPosition → {azimuth,altitude,distance(km),parallacticAngle};getMoonTimes → {rise?,set?}。

api_surface.sun-times: getTimes(date, lat, lng, height?) → SunTimes;可 addTime(angleDeg, riseName, setName) 追加自定义时刻
api_surface.sun-position: getPosition(date, lat, lng) → {azimuth(度,北基顺时针), altitude(度)}

peer.note: suncalc 出『某时某地』的具体时刻与方位/高度角,d3-celestial 画星空/天球图;想在星图上标太阳月亮位置或做日照分析,由本条供数、d3-celestial 供图。

boot.note: v2.0.1 是命名导出、无 default(import { getTimes, … })。角度是『度』、azimuth『北基顺时针』——务必按 v2 语义解读,别套 v1 弧度/南基旧记忆。极纬度下时刻可能为 null(alwaysUp/alwaysDown),渲染前判空。经纬度单位是度不是弧度。

isolation.evidence: Verified vs jsDelivr suncalc@2.0.1: +esm has NAMED exports only, no default — grep 'export{… as getPosition, … as getTimes, … as getMoonIllumination, … as getMoonPosition, … as getMoonTimes, … as addTime, … as times}'. Signatures from index.d.ts match boot: getPosition(date,lat,lng)→{azimuth,altitude}; getTimes(date,lat,lng,height?)→SunTimes; getMoonIllumination(date?)→{fraction,phase,angle,waxing}; getMoonPosition/getMoonTimes(date,lat,lng). UNITS CONFIRMED AGAINST REAL SOURCE (index.js), not just the .d.ts: line 42 comment 'north-based clockwise azimuth in degrees (0=N,90=E,180=S,270=W)' and lines 44/97 divide the result by rad(=PI/180) → outputs are DEGREES, north-based — this is the v2 breaking change vs v1's radians/south-based, so the schema note pins the correct v2 semantics. License: npm 'license' field empty, but gh LICENSE is verbatim BSD-2-Clause (2 numbered conditions, no endorsement clause). Pure numeric, no DOM/event/style footprint → trivially shadow-safe (headless-primitive, no carriers matrix by design).
